cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about what the International Olympic Committee (IOC) was concerned about regarding the 2008 Olympic medals that have jade rings. The passage mentions that the IOC was concerned about the risk of fragility when the jade ring design was proposed. They required the jade rings to be strong enough to withstand a 2-meter drop test. The mint even raised that to 3 meters. The answer should relate to the fragility of jade. Looking at the options: (A) the medals will be worth less – The passage doesn't mention anything about the monetary value. (B) the medals are too difficult to make – While the process is complex, the IOC's concern was about durability, not difficulty in making.
